title: Scientific Computing for Physics
text:
A modern introduction to Scientific Computing for Physics and Engineering students using Python.
Welcome to Scientific Computing for Physics, a long-term educational project that integrates scientific programming, data analysis, visualization, numerical methods, and Physics into a single learning path.
The goal of this project is to show that programming should not be learned separately from Physics, but rather as a tool for understanding physical phenomena, building computational models, and analyzing results.
This material is continuously developed and is intended to evolve over time into a complete resource for undergraduate students, educators, and researchers.

title: Scientific Computing for Physics
text:
Objectives
This project aims to:

Introduce Scientific Computing using Python.
Teach programming applied to Physics.
Develop skills in scientific data analysis and visualization.
Present numerical methods gradually and in context.
Complement traditional Physics teaching with computational experiments.
Provide an open and free educational resource.

title: Scientific Computing for Physics
text:
Continuous Development
This is a long-term project.
New notebooks, examples, simulations, and animations will be added continuously, covering the main topics of undergraduate Physics and Engineering.
The goal is to build, over time, a complete educational resource in Scientific Computing for Physics.
